name: shellcheck-configuration
description: Master ShellCheck static analysis configuration and usage for shell script quality. Use when setting up linting infrastructure, fixing code issues, or ensuring script portability.
capabilities: Configure ShellCheck for different shell dialects (bash, sh, dash, ksh)
Integrate ShellCheck into CI/CD pipelines and pre-commit hooks
Understand and fix common ShellCheck error codes (SC1000-SC3999)
Suppress false positives with inline directives
Use ShellCheck output formats (default, gcc, json, quiet)
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 111 static findings are false positives. The skill contains shell command examples used to demonstrate ShellCheck error codes (SC2086, SC1091, etc.). The scanner misidentified 'SC' as 'C2' keywords. URLs point to legitimate ShellCheck project resources. References to ~/.shellcheckrc and ~/.bashrc are standard config file paths, not malicious file access. The skill is purely educational documentation for ShellCheck static analysis tool.
valueStatement: Shell scripts often contain subtle bugs and portability issues that are hard to catch manually. This skill provides comprehensive guidance on configuring and using ShellCheck to automatically detect and fix shell script problems.
userTitle: Configure ShellCheck for Shell Script Quality
limitations: Requires ShellCheck to be installed on the system
Does not execute shell scripts - only performs static analysis
Cannot catch runtime logic errors or environmental issues
Limited to POSIX-compatible shell syntax analysis

bestPractices: Run ShellCheck in CI/CD pipelines to catch issues before merging
Configure the target shell correctly - do not analyze Bash scripts as POSIX sh
Document why specific warnings are suppressed using inline directives
antiPatterns: Disabling warnings without understanding why they appear
Analyzing scripts for wrong shell dialect (Bash as sh)
Ignoring ShellCheck output without addressing the underlying issue

faq.question: What is ShellCheck?
faq.answer: ShellCheck is a static analysis tool that finds bugs, warnings, and issues in shell scripts. It supports Bash, sh, dash, ksh, and other POSIX shells.

faq.question: How do I install ShellCheck?
faq.answer: On macOS: brew install shellcheck. On Ubuntu/Debian: apt-get install shellcheck. Or build from source at github.com/koalaman/shellcheck.

faq.question: What do ShellCheck error codes mean?
faq.answer: Codes are grouped: SC1000-1099 (parser errors), SC2000-2099 (shell issues), SC2100-2199 (quoting), SC3000-3999 (POSIX compliance). Each code has specific guidance.

faq.question: How do I suppress specific warnings?
faq.answer: Use inline directives: # shellcheck disable=SC2086. Or configure .shellcheckrc with disable=SC2086 to suppress project-wide.

faq.question: Can ShellCheck catch security issues?
faq.answer: ShellCheck detects some security-related patterns like unquoted variables, but it is not a security scanner. Use dedicated security tools for vulnerability assessment.

faq.question: How do I integrate ShellCheck with my editor?
faq.answer: Most editors have ShellCheck plugins: VS Code (vscode-shellcheck), Sublime Text, Vim (ale plugin), Emacs (flycheck). These provide real-time feedback.
